skill.name: github-issue-creator
skill.description: Convert raw notes, error logs, voice dictation, or screenshots into structured GitHub-flavored Markdown issue reports with summary, reproduction steps, impact, and context.
skill.summary: Turn messy bug details into clear GitHub issue drafts.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby shell backticks and weak cryptography are false positives caused by Markdown fences, Markdown examples, and .md naming text in SKILL.md. No malicious command execution, cryptographic code, exfiltration, or prompt injection evidence was found. The skill has low risk because it instructs the assistant to create issue Markdown files in an /issues/ directory.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to Markdown code fences and example Markdown output, not Ruby backtick execution. No shell command, interpreter invocation, or user-controlled command construction is present in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious characters are triple backtick Markdown delimiters around templates and examples. The file contains no Ruby code or executable command call at these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Text Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings are not cryptographic operations. They are ordinary issue-template text, Markdown references, and the .md filename convention used for issue files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines do not implement hashing, encryption, or authentication. The strongest evidence is the .md file extension on line 49, which is unrelated to MD5 or weak crypto.

low_findings.title: Repository File Creation Instruction
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to create issue reports as Markdown files in an /issues/ directory at the repository root. This is expected functionality, but it does require filesystem writes and should stay scoped to issue drafts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file creation instruction is explicit and matches the skill purpose. The risk is low because the target path and file type are narrow and no destructive operation is requested.

content.user_title: Create Clean GitHub Issues from Raw Notes
content.value_statement: Bug reports often start as scattered notes, logs, screenshots, or voice dictation. This skill converts that material into structured GitHub issue drafts that teams can triage quickly.
actual_capabilities: Formats raw bug details into GitHub-flavored Markdown issue reports.
Organizes reports into summary, environment, steps, expected behavior, actual behavior, impact, and context.
Extracts useful details from logs, informal notes, voice dictation, and screenshots.
Adds placeholders for missing or sensitive information.
Suggests impact severity based on the stated user or system effect.
Names issue files with a date and short description convention.
limitations: Does not submit issues to GitHub through the API.
Cannot verify facts that are missing from the user input.
May infer context from the conversation and should be reviewed before publication.
Requires the assistant to have permission before writing files in a repository.

use_cases.title: Convert Support Reports
use_cases.description: Turn customer bug reports into clear engineering issues with impact, reproduction steps, and missing fields marked.

use_cases.title: Prepare Developer Bug Tickets
use_cases.description: Transform copied logs and quick notes into issue drafts that are easier for maintainers to reproduce.

use_cases.title: Document Visual Defects
use_cases.description: Reference screenshots or GIFs in a structured issue so UI defects include visual evidence and expected behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic Bug Issue
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn these notes into a GitHub issue draft. Keep missing details as placeholders and include severity: [paste notes].

prompt_templates.title: Structure an Error Log
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a GitHub issue from this error log and short context. Include environment, reproduction steps, error details, and impact: [paste log and context].

prompt_templates.title: Use Screenshot Evidence
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a GitHub issue for this visual defect. Reference the attached screenshot, describe expected and actual behavior, and identify missing context.

prompt_templates.title: Create Repository Issue File
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this incident report into a polished issue file under /issues/ using the naming format YYYY-MM-DD-short-description.md. Redact sensitive identifiers.

output_examples.input: Voice note: The dashboard export button spins forever after I select a large date range. No error appears.
output_examples.output: A Markdown issue draft with a concise summary, dashboard environment placeholders, reproduction steps for selecting a large date range, expected export completion, actual endless spinner, and medium or high impact depending on workflow impact.

output_examples.input: Pasted log: 403 PERMISSION_DENIED when publishing an integration that worked yesterday.
output_examples.output: A structured issue that highlights the 403 error, captures the regression context, lists publish steps, requests missing region and version details, and marks the issue as high impact if publishing is blocked.

output_examples.input: Screenshot shows a checkout modal clipped on mobile Safari.
output_examples.output: A UI bug issue draft that references the screenshot attachment, records browser and device placeholders, describes the clipped modal, and states the expected responsive layout.

best_practices: Review inferred fields before sharing the issue with a team.
Replace sensitive values with placeholders before saving issue drafts.
Attach screenshots, logs, and reproduction details when available.
anti_patterns: Do not paste secrets, tokens, or customer data into issue text.
Do not let the skill invent product versions, regions, or reproduction steps.
Do not use generated severity without checking the real business impact.

faq.question: Does this skill create issues directly in GitHub?
faq.answer: No. It creates Markdown issue drafts and can save them as repository files when permitted.

faq.question: Can it work from screenshots?
faq.answer: Yes. It can reference screenshots or GIFs as visual evidence in the issue draft.

faq.question: Does it protect sensitive data?
faq.answer: It instructs the assistant to replace sensitive values with placeholders, but users should still review drafts carefully.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What output format does it use?
faq.answer: It produces GitHub-flavored Markdown with standard bug report sections.

faq.question: What should I provide for best results?
faq.answer: Provide the error, environment, observed behavior, expected behavior, reproduction steps, and any screenshots or logs.
